observations.managementAction: The Management committed to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
 Management justify the non-maintenance of Subsidiary Ledgers and non-submission of Notes to Financial Statements.
 Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant-Designate see to it that relevant records such as the Notes to Financial Statements and subsidiary ledgers are kept in sufficient details so as to facilitate better understanding as to the nature and composition of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed the recommendations (see Management’s Comment).

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to locate and submit the missing vouchers and other reports. The Municipal Accountant should see to it that monthly reports and journals be submitted to the auditor’s office together with the paid vouchers, payrolls and official receipts in accordance with the provisions of Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160 and COA Circular 2009-006 dated Sept. 2009.

observations.managementAction: The management committed to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended Management the following exert more effort to establish the existence and validity of the receivable accounts, and furnish COA with a copy of any relevant record containing sufficient information about the foregoing receivable balances in order to effect collection thereof. If not, Management may file request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts to COA following the procedures stated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We have recommended that Management direct the Accountant to review the composition of the Accounts Payable account to determine their validity and effect promptly the necessary accounting entries to revert all undocumented payables. Henceforth, refrain from setting up accounts payable unless the obligation is founded on a valid claim that is properly supported by sufficient evidence.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher as basis for recording in the General Journal. The Journal Entry Vouchers must be accompanied with proper supporting documents before the Municipal Accountant record the transactions. The Municipal Accountant should take extra care in recording of transactions.

We have recommended the management that all Memoranda of Agreement entered into by the municipality or Notice of Authority to Debit Account be submitted to the audit team to verify if the specific purpose to which the fund was established was met and justify the other observations noted. Also, for CY 2018 audit the NADAI and MOA for funds received from national government were not submitted.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare transmittal of submitted vouchers to the Municipal Accountant, who after recording in the Books of Accounts, will then submit the same to the Auditor for the conduct of audit so as to facilitate timely communication of noted deficiencies. This way, management could easily pinpoint the persons responsible and determine the causes of delay of submission of accounts and reports.

We have recommended that management prepare its APP and supporting PPMPs to serve as a guide in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects and furnish a copy of the same to COA for audit/evaluation purposes. No procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved APP of the procuring entity.
We have recommended that management form a Solid Waste Management Board that will prepare Solid Waste Management Plan that complies with Republic Act 9003.
We have recommended that the management justify the noted lapses and henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by the different rules and regulations cited above.

We have recommended that management establish their Local Council for the Protection of Children who shall prepare plans and ensure implementation of the same in compliance with the provisions of Section 15 of RA 9344 and DILG MC 2012-120.

We have recommended the following:
 That management submit written justification for the payments of infrastructures without the indispensable attachments;
 That management submit the lacking documents for evaluation and review; and
 Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it that all disbursement vouchers are properly supported with complete documentation before any payment is made.

We have recommended that management explain the noted lapses. Henceforth, avoid splitting of contracts and payments. Purchases beyond the threshold on shopping and small value items should be done through public bidding as mandated under R.A. No. 9184.

We have reiterated our recommendation that management should utilize the amount appropriated for capital outlay in procuring rescue and response equipment that can be used during calamity to mitigate the adverse effect of disasters and submit justification on why projects/programs/activities indicated in the plans were not followed. The unused capital outlay should be treated as continuing appropriations in the General Fund books.

We have recommended that the utilization of the 20% Development Fund be strictly limited to programs, projects and activities that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures and yield greater benefit to the people in accordance with DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1. Moreover, the management should submit the lacking documents for evaluation and review.

We have recommended that management submit justification as to why no payment for the labor component of the subject project has been made despite the supposed completion/implementation of the project/contract.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant–Designate submit the complete documentary requirements on the payments of pakyaw contracts. Henceforth, stop paying transactions that are not supported with complete documentation.

We have recommended that management stop the practice of granting cash advances without indicating its specific purpose. Submit liquidation of every cash advances granted for audit to avoid suspensions and disallowances.

We have recommended that due caution in recording and footing of transactions be observed by the Municipal Accountant and when errors are discovered, immediate corrections should be made. The Municipal Accountant should also observe accounting regulations in recording transactions in the books of accounts to ensure these are appropriately charged and present their correct balances in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Municipality establish a GAD database to serve as basis for planning, programming and formulation of gender related issues or concerns before the GAD Plan is finalized and submitted for review to the Municipal Planning and Development Office.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate trace the period or date of acquisition of the assets and compute the depreciation based on the estimated useful life of assets as provided under COA Circular 2003-007. Prior years’ depreciation should be taken up as Prior Years Adjustment to be closed to Government Equity and current year’s depreciation should be recorded as expense.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prioritize the preparation of the required monthly bank reconciliation statements to establish the actual and correct cash-in-bank balance of the agency. Henceforth, due caution in recording and footing of transactions should be observed by the Municipal Accountant and when errors are discovered, immediate corrections should be made. The Municipal Accountant should also observe accounting regulations in recording transactions in the books of accounts to ensure these are appropriately charged and present their correct balances in the financial statements. We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant incorporate the proposed adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the account.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to enforce submission of liquidation documents by the employees with outstanding cash advances. As such, withholding of their salaries could be considered pursuant to existing laws, rules and regulations; and stop granting Cash advances to Municipal Employees except for travelling expenses; Further, ensure that no additional cash advance be granted to any personnel who have outstanding cash advances.

We have recommended that the concerned officials strictly observe the rules and regulations prescribed in Section 361 of GAAM Volume I on the submission of mandatory reports, relative to the use of government vehicles and fuel consumption.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe proper documentation on the procurement of drugs and medicines in accordance of COA Circular No. 2012-001. Henceforth, ensure that all future procurements for medicines have complete documentation. Such documentation shall be duly accomplished bearing proper signatures, dates, places, names and other indispensable information.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive hire a qualified Municipal Accountant who shall take charge of both the accounting and internal audit services of the agency to improve the internal control system, financial reporting and operations of the Municipality.

We have recommended the following:
 That management submit written justification for the payments of infrastructures without the indispensable attachments;
 That management submit the lacking documents for evaluation and review; and
 Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it that all disbursement vouchers are properly supported with complete documentation before any payment is made.

We have recommended the following:
 That management submit list of beneficiaries with their addresses, quantity received and signatures to support the subject disbursements; and
 That management ensure that all assistance, whether in cash or in kind, be personally received by the intended beneficiaries to safeguard the resources so as to attain the purpose of the fund and in order to safeguard the resources of the government.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding VAT/NVAT/EWT on all its purchases and payments for contracts as mandated by laws, rules and regulations to avoid impositions of sanctions by the Bureau of Internal Revenue. We further recommend that purchases through cash or reimbursements should be minimized if not totally eliminated.

1.4.6 In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended that -
1.4.6.1 The Municipal Budget Officer-designate comply with the
requirements of properly accomplishing the Obligation
Requests for all expenditures. Ensure that the Registry of
Appropriations, Allotment and Obligations be maintained to
properly monitor the budget of the agency and serve as the
basis to prepare the SAAOB and SCBAA; and
1.4.6.2 The Municipal Accountant-designate not to process claims
without the required certification as to the availability of
appropriation including the specific cost centers or
function/program/project where expenses will be charged.
1.4.6.3 That Management submit to COA the CY 2018 Statement of
Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) for
audit. Henceforth, ensure the submission of complete
financial statements as required under Paragraph 21 of
PPSAS 1 to provide additional informative value to its users
for decision-making.

1.5.3 We have recommended that Management submit to COA the Notes
to the Financial Statement for CY 2018, for audit. Henceforth, ensure
the submission of complete financial statements as required under
Paragraph 21 of PPSAS 1 to provide additional informative value to
its users for decision-making.

2.11 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate and Acting
Municipal Treasurer 
2.11.1 submit justification for the noted lapses;
2.11.2 cause the immediate submission to COA the above disbursements
totaling ₱92,533,813.96, RCDs totaling ₱29,831.25, JEVs for CY 2018
as well as the lacking bank statements for audit purposes.
2.11.3 Henceforth, ensure the submission of financial statements, journals
and accounts within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 347 of
Republic Act No. 7160, Item 4 of COA Circular No. 2010-01 and
Paragraph 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 for timely audit and
communication of deficiencies.

3.8 We have recommended that the -
3.8.1 Municipal Engineer and the Municipal General Services Officer
conduct a yearly physical count of local road network projects and
accordingly update the local road map of the LGU;
3.8.2 Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant-designate and
Municipal General Services Officer the description and cost
segregation of road components of various road infrastructure
projects;
3.8.3 Municipal General Services Officer maintain a Local Road Inventory
and Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property
Card for all roads and its components;
3.8.4 The Municipal Accountant  designate -
3.8.4.1 trace the recorded road, highways and bridges which were
closed to government equity prior to 2016 and subsequently
record in its books of accounts the amount of road works
equivalent to the required transition provision;
3.8.4.2 keep and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Card for
roads and road components for every road network and
reconcile the same with the result of the physical inventory
taking at the end of the year. Also, provide for the
depreciation for the local road network and provide
disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements,

4.5 We have recommended that:
4.5.1 Management -
4.5.1.1 create an Inventory Committee that shall perform the
conduct of complete annual physical count of PPE in
accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for
LGUs, Vol. 1. A representative of the Auditor be invited to
witness the inventory taking for this purpose;
4.5.1.2 see to it that the Inventory Committee shall prepare the
Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE) which shall be reconciled with the
PPE records kept by the of the General Services Office and
Accounting Office; and that a copy of the RPCPPE shall be
submitted to COA for verification not later than January 31
of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the
NGAS;
4.5.2 The General Services Officer maintain Property Cards for PPE and
ARE/PAR and ensure that the ARE/PAR be renewed every after
three years pursuant to Section 492 of GAAM, Volume 1, to establish
effective management and control over PPE;
4.5.3 The Municipal Accountant-designate maintain complete and
updated PPELC for each category of PPE pursuant to Section 114 of
NGAS Manual for LGUs to provide ready information on the
accounts and to support balances in the financial statements.
4.5.4 The General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant-designate
reconcile their property records with the result of physical count to
ensure that PPE recorded in the property records really exists and
that all existing PPE are recorded in the books, in order to establish
correctness and completeness of balances of PPE accounts. Any
variance shall be dealt accordingly and subsequently adjusted as
necessary.
4.5.5 The Property and Accounting Units comply with the prescribed
procedures in COA Circular No. 89-296 on the disposal of
unserviceable PPE, if any.

5.12 We have recommended that Management 
5.12.1 Submit justification for the noted lapses;
5.12.2 Instruct the Municipal Accountant-designate to analyze the errors,
draw adjusting entries to correct account balances and present fairly
the financial statements. She should take extra care in recording
transactions, adhere to prescribed accounting rules and regulations in
order to attain fair and faithful presentation of financial statements.
5.12.3 Direct the Municipal Accountant-designate to maintain books of final
entry and complete books of original entry /journals to easily track
and determine balances and movement of accounts, its nature,
breakdown and details. The entries drawn in the specials journals
should be summarized and the individual entries in the GJ
subsequently posted in the General Ledgers (GL), which shall be the
bases of preparing the Financial Statements. Necessary information
in the Subsidiary Ledgers should be regularly updated to adequately
support the controlling account in the GL.
5.12.4 Moreover, it is highly recommended that LGU-Pidigan must hire a
Municipal Accountant pursuant to Section 474 of RA No. 7160, who
shall take charge of both the accounting and internal audit services of
the Municipality to improve its internal control system, financial
reporting and operations.

6.8 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate and Acting
Municipal Treasurer 
6.8.1 submit justification for the noted lapses;
6.8.2 cause the immediate submission to COA the above liquidations
totaling ₱5,185,562.52 including the lacking bank statements, for
audit purposes.
6.8.3 Henceforth, ensure the submission of accounts within the prescribed
period pursuant to Section 5.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002, and
Paragraph 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 for timely audit and
communication of deficiencies. Ensure that all recorded liquidations,
both in the cashbook and books of accounts, shall be duly supported
with report of disbursements or liquidation reports to establish its
validity.

7.7 We have recommended that Management 
7.7.1 Submit justification for the noted lapses. Henceforth, strictly abide by
the laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and
liquidation of cash advance.
7.7.2 Require the concerned officers and employees to immediately submit
liquidation papers. The liquidations documents be submitted
immediately to COA for verification.

8.1.13 We have recommended that Management -
8.1.13.1 submit justification for the noted lapses;
8.1.13.2 require the Municipal AccountantDesignate and BAC
Chairman to submit to COA the lacking documents
enumerated under Item 9.1.6 and 9.1.10 herein, respectively,
for audit purposes.
8.1.13.3 Henceforth, see to it that all disbursement vouchers and
contracts are duly documented pursuant to Section 4.6 of PD
1445 to establish legality of payments. Attending
payments/claims not properly documented be
discontinued.

8.2.6 We have recommended that Management 
8.2.6.1 require the conduct of materials testing for infrastructure
projects to uncover any possible inferior construction
materials used or components supplied by the contractor so
as to ensure the quality of projects; and that the results be
required before the payment of the contractors
accomplishments; and
8.2.6.2 Ensure that the provision on the conduct of material testing
and the submission of the test results as a requirement of
final payment be incorporated in the Special Condition of
the Contract.

8.3.7 We have recommended that the BAC Chairperson 
8.3.7.1 submit justification for the lapses noted;
8.3.7.2 in the future, see to it the prescribed 5th Edition or Latest
Philippine Bidding Documents and forms be adopted in all
the procurement process of the LGU pursuant to Section
17.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

8.4.7 We have recommended that Management submit justification on the
non-collection of the fees for bid documents. Henceforth, see to it that
the required fee stipulated in the Invitation to Bid be collected from
the participating bidder to recover the costs in the preparation and
bid documents.

8.5.4 We have recommended that Management 
8.5.4.1 submit explanation why the aforementioned projects were
paid before the Contractors Letter Request for payment and
issuance of Certificate of Completion; and
8.5.4.2 always ensure that the inspection and completion of projects
should be done before payment pursuant to Section 338 of RA
No. 7160 and Section 88 of PD No. 1445.

8.6.6 We have recommended that Management 
8.6.6.1 submit justification for the above noted lapses;
8.6.6.2 submit the surety bond or the substitute posted by the
contractor for the above releases of retention money, if any,
for verification; and
8.6.6.3 henceforth, ensure that the claim for the release of retention
money be supported by certificate of completion to easily
133
determine the end of 1-year defect liability. If the said 1-year
defect liability has yet to prescribe, require the contractor to
post a substitute in lieu of the released retention money by
any of the form prescribed in Section 6.2 of Annex E of the
2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

8.7.7 We have recommended that the Municipal Engineer 
8.7.7.1 provide clarification for the foregoing observations on the
computation of POW/Detailed Estimates;
8.7.7.2 Henceforth, be guided accordingly by latest and acceptable
estimating guidelines to ensure integrity of ABC and POW.

9.1.9 We have recommended that Management 
9.1.9.1 submit to COA a copy of the NADAI and its supporting
list of programs and projects covering the receipts of the
Share from Tobacco Excise Tax (RA 7171 and 8240) for
audit purposes; and
9.1.9.2 In the future, ensure to furnish COA copy of NADAI
received and its attachment to facilitate verification and
evaluation of the fund.

9.2.10 We have recommended that the -
9.2.10.1 Sanggunian Bayan pass a resolution authorizing the
LGU-Pidigan to open a separate depository bank
account for the Share from Tobacco Excise Tax.
9.2.10.2 Municipal Accountant-designate maintain separate
books of accounts for Tobacco Excise Tax fund as well
as establish a complete set of subsidiary ledgers to
account for its transactions for more effective
monitoring of utilization thereof.

9.1.9 We have recommended that Management 
9.1.9.1 submit to COA a copy of the NADAI and its supporting
list of programs and projects covering the receipts of the
Share from Tobacco Excise Tax (RA 7171 and 8240) for
audit purposes; and
9.1.9.2 In the future, ensure to furnish COA copy of NADAI
received and its attachment to facilitate verification and
evaluation of the fund.

9.2.10 We have recommended that the -
9.2.10.1 Sanggunian Bayan pass a resolution authorizing the
LGU-Pidigan to open a separate depository bank
account for the Share from Tobacco Excise Tax.
9.2.10.2 Municipal Accountant-designate maintain separate
books of accounts for Tobacco Excise Tax fund as well
as establish a complete set of subsidiary ledgers to
account for its transactions for more effective
monitoring of utilization thereof.

10.1.9 We have recommended that Management -
10.1.9.1 Submit to COA copy of the MOA, SB Resolution and
Program of Work or Project Proposal for the above-
mentioned fund transfer for verification purposes; and
10.1.9.2 Henceforth, all transactions involving fund transfer
from other local government units be fully documented
in accordance with the COA Circular 2012-001.

10.2.8 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant -
10.2.8.1 maintain separate subsidiary record for each fund
transfer received to provide complete information and
proper control on the utilization of the fund. Use sub-
accounts to facilitate easy identification of related assets
and liabilities; and
10.2.8.2 In the future, see to it that the year-end FS balance of
Due to LGUs account be supported with subsidiary
schedule and disclosed in the Notes to the FS to provide
more informative and useful data to the users of the
report.

11.10 We have recommended that Management submit to the audit team a
copy of the covering NADAIs and its supporting list of projects and the
above DVs, for audit purposes.

12.10 We have recommended that Management -
12.10.1 Submit to COA copy of the MOA and Work and Financial Plan,
SB Resolution and Program of Work or Project Proposal for the
above-mentioned fund transfer for verification purposes; and
12.10.2 Direct the Municipal Accountant to reclass the entry made to take
up the fund transfer, from Due to GOCCs to Due to NGAs;
12.10.3 Henceforth, all transactions involving fund transfer from national
government be fully documented in accordance with the COA
Circular 2012-001.

13.6 We have recommended that Management -
13.6.1 cause the submission of the duly certified and approved DTRs,
application for leave, and other necessary documents as the case
maybe, to COA for verification, and to support the payments of
the salaries of municipal officials and employees for CY 2018. For
the Vice Mayor and SB Members, copy of attendance and minutes
of sessions, as well as the copy of administered Oath of Office for
the ABC and SK Federation Presidents be submitted.
13.6.2 Henceforth, direct the Municipal Treasurerdesignate to ensure
that all payrolls be supported with duly certified and approved
DTR and other pertinent documents to support payments of
salary. Refrain from releasing the payment of salary to the
concerned municipal official/employee unless the afore-mentioned
requirements are submitted.

14.10 We have recommended that Management -
14.10.1 submit the following to support the validity of the claims:
a) Justification for the noted lapses;
b) Sanggunian Bayan Resolution authorizing the LCE to enter
into such Job Orders for CY 2018;
c) Certified copies of Job Order contracts from the Human
Resource Management Officer(HRMO), along with the
statement of expected work or output;
d) Verified Accomplishment Reports and approved DTRs;
14.10.2 Henceforth, make sure that Job Order appointees be backed up
by the required SB Resolution and covering contracts and that
the daily wage payrolls be supported with duly accomplished
Obligation Requests, approved DTRs and validated
accomplishment reports before payment is effected to establish
the validity of the transactions.

15.1.7 We have recommended that Management see to it that fully
accomplished Drivers Trip Tickets be prepared to ensure proper
use of government vehicle and consumption of fuel, summary of
which shall be prepared at the end of each month in a Monthly
Report of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption and
submit to this Office for audit/evaluation. Also, we reiterate the
submission of RPCPPE or list of motor vehicle and heavy
equipment own by LGU-Pidigan, for audit purposes.

15.2.4 We have recommended that -
15.2.4.1 Management submit justification for the lapses noted.
Also, furnish the Audit Team a copy of budget realignment
and/or supplemental budget, if any, for audit purposes.
Henceforth, spend within the limit of approved
appropriations.
15.2.4.2 The Municipal Accountant-designate see to it all claims be
supported with duly accomplished Obligations Request
and maintain the registries required under Section 7 of the
Manual on NGAS, Volume 1 to monitor and control
incurrence of obligations.

16.10 We have recommended that -
16.10.1 Management submit the above-mentioned lacking documents
and or provide the needed information to support the validity of
the disbursements;
16.10.2 Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant-designate prepare and
attach checklist of documentary requirements for every
transaction/DV and ensure that all required documents were
submitted, accomplished and properly reviewed. Attending
payment of claims that are not sufficiently documented be
discontinued.

17.7 We have recommended that Management -
17.7.1 submit the APP and supporting PPMPs for CY 2018 to COA
for audit purposes, if any;
17.7.2 henceforth, prepare Annual Procurement Plan which serves as a
guide on their procurement activities pursuant to Section 7.2 of
the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Instruct every office to prepare
their PPMP for consolidation to APP. A copy thereof be
furnished to COA for audit purposes.

18.6.1 We have recommended that the BAC Chairman see to it that complete
Bidding Documents, all Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed, and
other related procurement-notices be posted in the PhilGEPS portal.
Make sure to fully use the system in accordance with pertinent provisions
of RA No. 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR and GPPB issuances.

19.7 We have recommended that Management justify the noted lapses.
Henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions on the information
and publicity of programs and projects set forth under COA Circular
COA Circular No. 2013-004.

20.1.10 We have recommended that Management -
20.1.10.1 submit justification for the noted lapses;
20.1.10.2 require the Municipal AccountantDesignate and
MPDC-designate/BAC Chairman to submit to COA the
lacking documents enumerated under Item 1.8, for
audit purposes.
20.1.10.3 Henceforth, see to it that all disbursement vouchers be
properly documented pursuant to Section 4.6 of PD
1445 to establish legality of the transactions. Payments
of claims not properly documented should be
discontinued.

20.2.11 We have recommended that Management -
20.2.11.1 submit justification for the lapses noted;
20.2.11.2 adhere with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No.
2017-1 on the appropriation and utilization of the 20%
of the IRA for programming of development projects.
Consider utilizing the fund for projects that yield
lasting benefit to the constituents of the Municipality;
and
20.2.11.3 In the future, instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to
provide the specific details of lump sum appropriation
in the 20% DF Annual Investment Plan to expedite
implementation of development projects.

20.3.7 We have recommended that Management -
20.3.7.1 Submit explanation for the above non-implementation of
priority projects; and
20.3.7.2 follow programmed timelines for period of
implementation in the AIP to ensure the full
implementation of the development projects and best
utilization of available of resources. Prepare project
implementation schedule in order to effectively
implement and monitor development projects of the
municipality.

21.4 We have recommended that -
21.4.1 Management submit justification for the noted lapses;
21.4.2 henceforth, the Municipal Accountant-designate and Local
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO)
submit the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of the
DRRMF to COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each
month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

22.1.3 We have recommended that Management allocate at least 5% of
the total annual budget for the implementation of GAD-related
activities as required under Section 30 of the General Provisions
of the General Appropriations Act of FY 2018 or RA No. 10964.

22.2.5 We have recommended that the Municipality observe the
scheduled GAD Planning and Budgeting as provided by PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by PCW-DILG-
DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 to facilitate the timely
submission to PPDO and the subsequent review and
endorsement by the DILG Provincial Office.

22.3.5 We have recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal
Person to furnish COA a copy GAD Accomplishment Report and
its accompanying documents duly received by PPDO and DILG
Provincial Office for audit purposes. Henceforth, see to it GAD
AR be submitted to the assigned Audit Team within five (5)
working days from the end of the January of the preceding year
pursuant to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001.

23.4 We have recommended that Management -
23.4.1 submit the approved CY 2018 Municipal School Board Budget, if
any, and/or justification why such expenses were paid without the
corresponding budget.
23.4.2 Henceforth, follow strictly the provisions of Section 99 of R.A. No.
7160 and Article 184 of the IRR of the Local Government Code
before the disbursement of the said fund.

24.9 We have recommended that Management -
24.10 Submit written explanation for the noted lapses; and or require the
contractor to submit proof that he has paid the corresponding
withholding taxes, as the case may be.
24.11 Henceforth, deduct, withhold and remit the prescribed
withholding taxes on government money payments as required
under pertinent Revenue Regulations.

25.12 We have recommended that Management -
25.12.1 submit clarification for the lapses noted;
25.12.2 submit a copy of the Alpha List of Employees for CY 2018 duly
received by BIR, as well as proof of payments of refund for the
excessive WTC, if any, for audit purposes;
25.12.3 Henceforth, compute, withheld and deduct taxes accordingly
from the salaries of employees, pursuant to Section 24 (2) of
NIRC as amended by R. A. No. 10963 and other pertinent
provisions thereof. Compute year-end adjustments for all
employees to ensure that the tax withheld would be equivalent to
tax due, and that additional deduction or refund be effected, as
necessary.

26.1 We have recommended that Management enforce settlements of the
above suspensions and disallowances.
We have recommended that the management promptly conduct an inventory of its road network system and the GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer said they are in the process of updating their inventory of road networks and road map. He committed to submit as soon as they have completed their reports.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
 That the Sanggunian Bayan pass a resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate bank account for the unexpended balance of LDRRMF;
 That the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF to the Trust Fund to be opened; and
 That the Municipal Accountant should maintain a special account and a complete set of subsidiary for the said fund and ensure that duly accomplished obligation requests should be attached to every voucher.

We have recommended that Management –
 in cases of lump sum appropriations containing general terms, a separate SB appropriate ordinance/resolution be secured by the Municipal Mayor to serve as an authority to enter into contract in behalf of the Municipal Government in accordance with Section 287 (c) of R.A. No. 7160 and the afore-cited jurisprudence. To eliminate the need for a separate authorization and in order to expedite the implementation of future development projects, we strongly recommend that the PPAs to be included in the Annual Investment Plan be specifically identified and be fully described in detail in the AIP.
 ensure regular transfer of the amount pertaining to the 20% Development Fund to its respective bank account so as to increase efficiency in the monitoring of the allocation and utilization of the fund.

observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person submitted on June 26, 2019, the GAD Plan and Budget and Certificate of Review issued by DILG Abra Provincial Office, stating that the GAD Plan and Budget of the LGU-Pidigan was “Fully compliant”. The Management committed to comply with recommendations regarding documentation and OBRs.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipality’s GAD Focal Point prepare the annual and performance-based LGU GPB in response to the gender issues and or concerns of the locality and in the context of the LGU mandate; and consolidate the same following the form and procedures prescribed in the Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC).

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer locate and submit the required documents. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that monthly reports and journals should be submitted to the auditor’s office together with the paid vouchers /liquidations/payrolls /official receipts within ten days after the end of each month.

We have recommended that the disbursement made from the fund should be recorded first as asset or expense account and later closed to the Due to Other NGAs after securing the Credit Notice. Review of the account including prior years’ transactions be made to establish the correct composition of the Due to National Government Agency account. The Municipal Accountant should maintain a subsidiary ledger so that adequate information could be provided when needed and to ascertain the composition of the balance of the account at year end. Furthermore, funds received with specific purpose should have been transferred and disbursed in the Trust Fund Account.

We have recommended that all procurement shall be meticulously planned and that the same shall be within the approved budget of the LGU. Resort to the reimbursement method of procurement shall be used sparingly and only in cases of extreme emergency and some other unforeseen and unavoidable situations. We have also recommended that management ensure proper and complete documentation of all their future procurements in accordance with the requirements of existing laws, rules and regulations.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate trace the period or date of acquisition of the assets and compute the depreciation based on the estimated useful life of assets as provided under COA Circular 2003-007. Prior years’ depreciation should be taken up as Prior Years Adjustment to be closed to Government Equity and current year’s depreciation should be recorded as expense.
observations.reason: Still no depreciation provided.

observations.recommendation:

Henceforth, due caution in recording and footing of transactions should be observed by the Municipal Accountant and when errors are discovered, immediate corrections should be made. The Municipal Accountant should also observe accounting regulations in recording transactions in the books of accounts to ensure these are appropriately charged and present their correct balances in the financial statements.
We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant incorporate the proposed adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the account.

Monthly bank reconciliations were not meticulously prepared, prepared,
Not Implemented. The Municipal Accountant incorporate the proposed adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the account.

Submit liquidations for audit to avoid suspensions and disallowances.

No. 7160 and the afore-cited jurisprudence. The unused capital outlay should be treated as continuing appropriations in the General Fund books.

observations.recommendation: We appreciate the agency for the integration in its budgeting the programs and activities that address gender issues. However, we have recommended that the projects included in the plan should be methodically considered and fully implemented to fully address the needs of the constituents.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding VAT/NVAT/EWT on all its purchases and payments for contracts as mandated by laws, rules and regulations to avoid impositions of sanctions by the Bureau of Internal Revenue. We further recommend that purchases through cash or reimbursements should be minimized if not totally eliminated.

10.1 The Budget Officer and Accountant justify the non-maintenance of
the required registries, failure to duly accomplish the required
obligation request as well as the non-submission of SAAOB;
10.2 The Budget Officer coordinate accordingly with the Accountant in
the preparation and submission of the SCBAA the soonest possible
time so as to allow the audit team to have ample time to verify its
validity;
10.3 The Budget Officer and different department heads strictly adhere
to the budgetary and disbursement process; and
10.4 Henceforth, management familiarize themselves and ensure strict
compliance with the different budgetary requirements.

• Management justify the non-maintenance of Subsidiary Ledgers and non-
submission of Notes to Financial Statements
• Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant Designate see to it that relevant
records such as the Notes to Financial Statements and subsidiary ledgers
are kept in sufficient details so as to facilitate better understanding as to the
nature and composition of accounts.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Treasurer to locate and submit the missing vouchers and
other reports. The Municipal Accountant should see to it that monthly reports
and journals be submitted to the auditor’s office together with the paid
vouchers, payrolls and official receipts in accordance with the provisions of
Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160 and COA Circular 2009-006 dated Sept.
2009.

• Management create an Inventory Committee that will perform the
conduct of complete annual physical count of PPE in accordance with
Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Vol. 1. A
representative of the Auditor be invited to witness the inventory taking
for this purpose;
• the Property and Accounting Units maintain complete and accurate
PPELC for each category of PPE pursuant to Section 114 on the NGAS
Manual for LGUs in order to promote effective monitoring and controls
over PPE;
• a reconciliation of the Property and Accounting records be done after the
conduct of physical count;
• the Inventory Committee submit the Physical Inventory Reports to the
Property Officer and COA Office for verification and reconciliation of
the result of the physical inventory with the complete Property, Plant and
Equipment Ledger Cards and the Property Cards maintained by the
Property and Accounting Units;
• ARE/PAR be maintained and renewed every three years in accordance
with the Instructions on the use of ARE/PAR;
• the Property and Accounting Units comply with the prescribed
procedures in COA Circular No. 89-296 on the disposal of unserviceable
PPE, if any; and
• Management transfer to the Department of Education the cost of the
school building through a Journal Entry Voucher to be drawn by the
Municipal Accountant and copy furnished the Accountant of DepEd.

We have recommended that the management promptly conduct an inventory
of its road network system and the GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory
and Road Map.

We have recommended Management the following exert more effort to
establish the existence and validity of the receivable accounts, and furnish
COA with a copy of any relevant record containing sufficient information about the foregoing receivable balances in order to effect collection thereof. If
not, Management may file request for authority to write-off dormant
receivable accounts to COA following the procedures stated in COA Circular
No. 2016-005.

We have recommended that Management direct the Accountant to review the
composition of the Accounts Payable account to determine their validity and
effect promptly the necessary accounting entries to revert all undocumented
payables. Henceforth, refrain from setting up accounts payable unless the
obligation is founded on a valid claim that is properly supported by sufficient
evidence.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry
Voucher as basis for recording in the General Journal. The Journal Entry
Vouchers must be accompanied with proper supporting documents before the
Municipal Accountant record the transactions. The Municipal Accountant
should take extra care in recording of transactions.

We have recommended the management that all Memoranda of Agreement
entered into by the municipality or Notice of Authority to Debit Account be
submitted to the audit team to verify if the specific purpose to which the fund
was established was met and justify the other observations noted.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare transmittal of
submitted vouchers to the Municipal Accountant, who after recording in the
Books of Accounts, will then submit the same to the Auditor for the conduct of
audit so as to facilitate timely communication of noted deficiencies. This way,
management could easily pinpoint the persons responsible and determine the
causes of delay of submission of accounts and reports.

We have recommended that management prepare its APP and supporting
PPMPs to serve as a guide in the procurement of goods and infrastructure
projects and furnish a copy of the same to COA for audit/evaluation purposes.
No procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the
approved APP of the procuring entity.

We have recommended that management form a Solid Waste Management
Board that will prepare Solid Waste Management Plan in compliance with
Republic Act 9003.

We have recommended that the management justify the noted lapses and
henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by the different
rules and regulations cited above.

We have recommended that management establish their Local Council for the
Protection of Children who shall prepare plans and ensure implementation of
the same in compliance with the provisions of Section 15 of RA 9344 and DILG
MC 2012-120.

• That management submit written justification for the payments of
infrastructures without the indispensable attachments;
• That management submit the lacking documents for evaluation and
review; and
• Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it that all disbursement
vouchers are properly supported with complete documentation before
any payment is made.

We have recommended that management explain the noted lapses.
Henceforth, avoid splitting of contracts and payments. Purchases beyond the
threshold on shopping and small value items should be done through public
bidding as mandated under R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR.

● That the Sanggunian Bayan pass a resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor
and the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate bank account for the
unexpended balance of LDRRMF;
● That the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer transfer the
unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF to the Trust Fund to be
opened; and
● That the Municipal Accountant should maintain a special account and a
complete set of subsidiary for the said fund and ensure that duly
accomplished obligation requests should be attached to every voucher.

We have reiterated our recommendation that management should utilize the
amount appropriated for capital outlay in procuring rescue and response
equipment that can be used during calamity to mitigate the adverse effect of
disasters and submit justification on why projects/programs/activities
indicated in the plans were not followed. The unused capital outlay should be
treated as continuing appropriations in the General Fund books.

19.2.7 We have recommended that Management, in cases of lump sum
appropriations containing general terms, a separate SB appropriation
ordinance/resolution be secured by the Municipal Mayor to serve as an
authority to enter into contract in behalf of the Municipal Government
in accordance with Section 287 (c) of R.A. No. 7160 and the afore-cited
jurisprudence. To eliminate the need for a separate authorization and
in order to expedite the implementation of future development projects,
we strongly recommend that the PPAs to be included in the Annual
Investment Plan be specifically identified and be fully described in
detail in the AIP.
19.2.8 We have also recommended that management ensure regular transfer of the amount
pertaining to the 20% Development Fund to its respective bank account so as to
increase efficiency in the monitoring of the allocation and utilization of the fund.
19.3.4 We have recommended that the utilization of the 20% Development
Fund be strictly limited to programs, projects and activities that will
contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and
environmental management outcomes and shall partake the nature of
investment or capital expenditures and yield greater benefit to the
people in accordance with DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular
No. 2017-1. Moreover, the management should submit the lacking
documents for evaluation and review.
19.4.3 We have recommended that management submit justification as to why
no payment for the labor component of the subject project has been
made despite the supposed completion/implementation of the
project/contract.
19.5.3 We have recommended that the Municipal AccountantDesignate
submit the complete documentary requirements on the payments of
pakyaw contracts. Henceforth, stop paying transactions that are not
supported with complete documentation.
19.6.4 We have recommended that management stop the practice of granting
cash advances without indicating its specific purpose. Submit
liquidation of every cash advances granted for audit to avoid
suspensions and disallowances.
19.7.5 We have recommended that due caution in recording and footing of
transactions be observed by the Municipal Accountant and when errors
are discovered, immediate corrections should be made. The Municipal
Accountant should also observe accounting regulations in recording
transactions in the books of accounts to ensure these are appropriately
charged and present their correct balances in the financial statements.

● That the agency integrate in its budgeting an amount equal to 5% of the
total appropriation for PPAs that address gender issues;
● That a Gender and Development Plan be prepared containing the specific
projects and activities addressing gender and development and equity for
women and the utilization should be in accordance with the GAD Plan;
● Ensure that Obligation Requests (OBRs) be duly accomplished and
required registries of allotment and obligations are maintained; and
● Ensure that Accomplishment Reports contain the actual accomplishment
vis-vis targets as well as the amounts utilized and a list of expenditures
that are related with the utilization of the said fund.

We have recommended that the municipalitys GAD Focal Point prepare the
annual and performance-based LGU GPB in response to the gender issues and
or concerns of the locality and in the context of the LGU mandate; and
consolidate the same following the form and procedures prescribed in the Joint
Memorandum Circular (JMC).

We have recommended that the Municipality establish a GAD database to
serve as basis for planning, programming and formulation of gender related
issues or concerns before the GAD Plan is finalized and submitted for review
to the Municipal Planning and Development Office.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer locate and submit the required documents. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that monthly reports and journals should be submitted to the auditor’s office together with the paid vouchers/liquidations/payrolls/official receipts within ten days after the end of each month.
observations.reason: Some of the vouchers were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor should create a team to conduct a complete physical inventory taking of property and supplies of the Municipal Government to establish the existence and actual condition of the assets of the agency. Also, the Municipal Accountant should maintain PPELC to be reconciled with the balances of PPE per General Ledger and Property Cards kept by the Property Custodian.

We have recommended that copies of Transfer Certificate of Title and other supporting documents pertaining to the above properties should be submitted to the Office of the Auditor to validate ownership and for us to recommend proper adjusting entry to be made to take up the market value of the Land in the books of the municipality in accordance with Section 54 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual.

In view of the foregoing, we have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prioritize the preparation of the required monthly bank reconciliation statements to establish the actual cash-in-bank balance of the agency.

We also recommended requiring the Municipal Accountant to incorporate the proposed adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the account.

We recommend that review of the account including prior years’ transactions should be made to establish the correct composition of the Due to National Government Agency account. The Municipal Accountant should maintain subsidiary ledger so that adequate information could be provided when needed and to ascertain balance of account at year end.

We have  recommended that:

the Municipal Treasurer should submit justification for the over and under remittance of taxes. 
the Municipal Accountant should prepare a schedule of taxpayers with the corresponding taxes withheld based on the disbursement vouchers covering payments to contractors and suppliers before the Municipal Treasurer remit to the Bureau of Internal Revenue
the Municipal Treasurer should see to it that amount withheld should be the amount remitted and comply with Section 4.2 of Revenue Regulations No. 6-2001 to avoid incurrence of penalties.
The Municipal Accountant should trace the transactions that resulted to a cumulative balance of P 288,161.84 balance from previous years, and remit and effect necessary adjustment to present fairly the balance of Due to BIR;
The Municipal Accountant-Designate and Municipal Treasurer should deduct from the salaries of the concerned employees, the taxes remitted but not deducted from their salaries.
The Municipal Accountant record the following entry:

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections regularly and to deposit the balance of ₱147,682.34 as of December 31, 2015and henceforth abide by the rules and regulations on deposit of collections pursuant to Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS, Section 111 of GAAM Vol. 1 and DBM-DOF Joint Cir. 1-81.
We have recommended that Municipal Accountant submit the required complete set of financial statements to enable the audit team to render an opinion.

We have recommended that review of the account including prior years’ transactions should be made to establish the correct composition of the Due to National Government Agency account. The Municipal Accountant should maintain a subsidiary ledger so that adequate information could be provided when needed and to ascertain the composition of the balance of the account at year end. Furthermore, funds received with specific purpose should have been transferred and disbursed in the Trust Fund Account.

We have recommended that all SARO and Memoranda Agreement entered into by the municipality be submitted to the audit team to verify if the specific purpose to which the funds was established was met. Disbursement made from the fund should be recorded first as asset or expense account and later closed to the Due to Other NGAs after securing Credit Notice.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to locate and submit the missing documents.  Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that monthly reports and journals should be submitted to the auditor’s office together with the paid vouchers /liquidations/payrolls/official receipts within ten days after the end of each month.

We have recommended that management explain the reason of incurring additional government contributions in excess of the prescribed limits. We also recommend that the excess employer’s contribution should be refunded by the employees concerned since the amount already formed part of their contribution to the HDMF and will be refunded to them.

In view of the above noted observations, we have recommended that Municipal Officials refrain from using the reimbursement method of payment for supplies unless there are unforeseen events which will justify the immediate payment thereof.

We have recommended that in cases of lump sum appropriations containing general terms, a separate SB appropriation ordinance/resolution must always be secured by the Municipal Mayor to serve as an authority to enter into contract in behalf of the Municipal Government in accordance with Section 22 of RA No. 7160.

We have recommended that Supplemental Budget should be prepared and approved before the share in tobacco excise tax is disbursed. Specific programs, projects and activities to be implemented should be indicated in the supplemental budget for the audit team to verify if such funds were used for authorized projects.

We have recommended the following: 
that expenditures should be limited to what is appropriated. 
the Municipal Accountant or the Budget Officer should maintain the required registries to prevent the incurrence of expenditures in excess of allotments. 
that the municipal budget should be carefully prepared and reviewed so that actual needs of the municipality are properly allocated in the budget. 
that the Obligation Requests which is the basis for recording of obligations in the registries should be properly accomplished and the four RAAOs should be maintained and updated to facilitate monitoring of expenses and balances of appropriations.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive hire a qualified Municipal Accountant who shall take charge of both the accounting and internal audit services of the agency to improve the internal control system, financial reporting and operations of the Municipality.

We have recommended to management that copy of the contracts should be furnished to the Auditor within five (5) working days from perfection as  required under  COA Circular 2009-001,  together  with  the  bidding  documents,  BAC  resolution,  minutes  of  the  proceedings,  abstracts,  and such other documents peculiar to the contract  and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial and technical review.

In view of the above noted lapses, we have recommended the following:

The Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant and Treasurer justify the payment of the transactions despite the absence of the BAC Resolution recommending the mode of procurement used and award of the contract
The management justify the non-conduct of competitive public bidding.
We have recommended that the municipality should avoid splitting of purchases and payments. Procurement with total cost beyond the threshold should be done through public bidding.

We have recommended that the Municipality comply with the provisions of RA 9184. We also have recommended that the members of the BAC attend trainings and updates on the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.

We recommend that progress payments should be subjected to the ten percent (10%) retention or substituted with other allowable form of guarantees to protect the interest of the municipality in case of defects in implemented projects or from third party liabilities.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the 20% of the Internal Revenue Allotment shall be utilized at optimum to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcome in compliance with the DILG DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1. Further, the local chief executive shall evaluate  the status of implementation of development projects and recommend the reversion of the unutilized funds for re-appropriation to other  priority development projects identified to be funded under the 20% Development Fund. The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator should monitor and ensure that the projects are implemented as planned.
We have recommended that the LGU should conform to the design standards for FMRs to ensure the quality and safety of the constructed roads.

We have recommended that Management justify the above-noted lapses. Utilization of the 20% Development Fund should be limited to programs, projects and activities that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures and yield greater benefit to the people in accordance with DBM-DILG Joint memorandum Circular No. 2011-1. The Municipal Accountant – designate should see to it that all disbursement vouchers are properly supported with complete documentation. The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator should monitor and ensure that the projects under the Annual Investment Plan are implemented as planned and revise the plan in cases projects included in the plan do not satisfy the purpose of the 20% Development Fund or if there are projects which management deemed to be given priority.

We have recommended that the MDRRMO should follow the prescribed format of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Investment Plan to properly address the concerns pertaining to the purpose of the fund and to ensure the preparedness of the Municipality to address or mitigate the effect of calamities.

We have recommended that Management should strictly comply with the provisions of RA 10121 and the related subsequent provisions on LDRRMF utilization. The LDRRMF should be strictly utilized for the implementation of DRRM programs. 

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should comply with the regulations on the transfer of unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF to the Trust Fund. We have recommended that the Sanggunian Bayan Members should pass a board resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate trust fund bank account for the unexpended balance of LDRRMF.  Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant should maintain a special account and a complete set of subsidiary for the said funds. The unused capital outlay should be treated as continuing appropriations in the General Fund books.

We have recommended that management should have utilized the amount appropriated for capital outlay for procuring rescue and response equipment that can be used during calamity to mitigate the adverse effect of disasters.

We have recommended that the agency should integrate in its budgeting, programs and activities that address gender issues. A Gender and Development Plan should be prepared containing the specific projects and activities addressing gender and development and equity for women and the utilization should be in accordance with the GAD Plan. Accomplishment Report should be prepared in the required format. Projects included in the plan should be implemented to fully address the needs of the constituents.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer locate and submit the required documents.  Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that monthly reports and journals should be submitted to the auditor’s office together with the paid vouchers /liquidations/payrolls/official receipts within ten days after the end of each month.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate trace the period or date of acquisition of the assets and compute the depreciation based on the estimated useful life of assets as provided under COA Circular 2003-007.  Prior years’ depreciation should be taken up as Prior Years Adjustment to be closed to Government Equity and current year’s depreciation should be recorded as expense.
We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and submit the bases in the preparation of the financial statements.

We have recommended that:
Management promptly conduct an inventory of its road network system and the GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
The Municipal Accountant trace the recorded road, highways and bridges which were closed to government equity in the prior years and subsequently record in its books of accounts the amount of road works equivalent to the required transition provision.

We have recommended the following:
The Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to enforce submission of liquidation documents by the employees with outstanding cash advances.  As such, withholding of their salaries could be considered pursuant to existing laws, rules and regulations; and
Stop granting Cash advances to Municipal Employees except for travelling expenses;  Further, ensure that no additional cash advance be granted to any personnel who have outstanding cash advances.
Management stop the practice of granting cash advances with no specific purpose. Submit liquidations for audit to avoid suspensions and disallowances.

We have recommended that review of the account including prior years’ transactions be made to establish the correct composition of the Due to National Government Agency account. The Municipal Accountant should maintain a subsidiary ledger so that adequate information could be provided when needed and to ascertain the composition of the balance of the account at year end. Furthermore, funds received with specific purpose should have been transferred and disbursed in the Trust Fund Account.
We have also recommended that all SARO and Memoranda Agreement entered into by the municipality be submitted to the audit team for verification if the specific purpose to which the funds was established was met. Disbursement made from the fund should be recorded first as asset or expense account and later closed to the Due to Other NGAs after securing Credit Notice.

We have recommended that the concerned officials strictly observe the rules and regulations prescribed in Section 361 of GAAM Volume I on the submission of mandatory reports, relative to the use of government vehicles and fuel consumption.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe proper documentation on the procurement of drugs and medicines in accordance of COA Circular No. 2012-001. Henceforth, ensure that all future procurements for medicines have complete documentation.  Such documentation shall be duly accomplished bearing proper signatures, dates, places, names and other indispensable information.

In view of the above noted observations, we have recommended that all procurement shall be meticulously planned and that the same shall be within the approved budget of the LGU.  Resort to the reimbursement method of procurement shall be used sparingly and only in cases of extreme emergency and some other unforeseen and unavoidable situations.
We have also recommended that management ensure proper and complete documentation of all their future procurements in accordance with the requirements of existing laws, rules and regulations.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive hire a qualified Municipal Accountant who shall take charge of both the accounting and internal audit services of the agency to improve the internal control system, financial reporting and operations of the Municipality.

We have recommended the following:
That management submit written justification for the payments of infrastructures without the indispensable attachments;
That management submit the lacking documents for evaluation and review; and
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it that all disbursement vouchers are properly supported with complete documentation before any payment is made.

We have recommended that management explain the noted lapses.  Henceforth, avoid splitting of contracts and payments.  Purchases beyond the threshold on shopping and small value items should be done through public bidding as mandated under R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR.

We have recommended that the Municipal Government shall, in the implementation of projects covered by a MOA, only implement projects embodied in the agreement. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should exercise due care in recording transactions. We also recommend that before payment is made, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that transactions were completely documented.

We have recommend that Management, in cases of lump sum appropriations containing general terms, a separate SB appropriation ordinance/resolution be secured by the Municipal Mayor to serve as an authority to enter into contract in behalf of the Municipal Government in accordance to Section 22 (c) of R.A. No. 7160 and the afore-cited jurisprudence. To eliminate the need for a separate authorization and in order to expedite the implementation of future development projects, we strongly recommend that the PPAs to be included in the Annual Investment Plan be specifically identified and be fully described in detail in the AIP.

We have recommended that the municipality adhere strictly with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 on the appropriations and utilization of the 20% of the Internal Revenue Allotment for development projects. Likewise, submit justification for implementing projects not in accordance with DILG DM JMC 2011-1.

We have recommended that management submit justification as to why no payment for the labor component of the subject project has been made despite the supposed completion/implementation of the project/contract.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant–Designate submit the complete documentary requirements on the payments of pakyaw contracts. Henceforth, stop paying transactions that are not supported with complete documentation.

We have recommended the following:
That the Sanggunian Bayan pass a resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate bank account for the unexpended balance of LDRRMF;
That the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF to the Trust Fund to be opened; and
That the Municipal Accountant maintains a special account and a complete set of subsidiary for the said fund.

In view of the foregoing observation, we have recommended the following:
That management submit list of beneficiaries with their addresses, quantity received and signatures to support the subject disbursements; and
That management ensure that all assistance, whether in cash or in kind, be personally received by the intended beneficiaries to safeguard the resources so as to attain the purpose of the fund and in order to safeguard the resources of the government.

We have recommended that management should have utilized the amount appropriated for capital outlay for procuring rescue and response equipment that can be used during calamity to mitigate the adverse effect of disasters and submit justification on why projects/programs/activities indicated in the plans were not followed. The unused capital outlay should be treated as continuing appropriations in the General Fund books.
We have recommended that the projects included in the plan should be methodically considered and fully implemented to fully address the needs of the constituents.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding VAT/NVAT/EWT on all its purchases and payments for contracts as mandated by laws, rules and regulations to avoid impositions of sanctions by the Bureau of Internal Revenue. We further recommend that purchases through cash or reimbursements should be minimized if not totally eliminated.
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant- Designate identify the details of the Public Infra account and close such accounts to Government Equity.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the SB authorization/resolution, contract of services/job orders and duly accomplished Obligation Requests as required under the aforementioned regulations.

We also have recommended that the Municipal Accountant evaluate and see to it that disbursement vouchers were supported with complete supporting documents before payment is effected to establish the validity of the transactions as well as the correctness of the accounts used.
